rationale: Both outcomes delivered. Outcome 1: ms-panel.js tracked at both locations, byte-identical (AC1 run: PASS), embedded via WorkflowFiles() and installed by RunClaude only (TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel, TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly both PASS). Outcome 2: ms-panel-run gains the Runner dispatch section reading `mindspec config show | grep '^runner:'`, workflow path = lens composition + one /ms-panel invocation with {slug,spec,target,bead_id?,round,lenses,mix,claude_sub_on_quota}; all four superseded sections gated with 'claude-code-skills path only' banners, not deleted; Slot lens defaults retained. Walked every R1-R9 falsification clause against the code: mix sourced only from args.mix (flattenMix); sha advisory-only (never read to set SHA); panel.json written only by `panel create` agent step; anti-laundering ladder correct in runCodexSlot (rendered parse-failure -> single same-reviewer re-serialize -> MISSING, never substituted; quota_wall-only substitution gated on claude_sub_on_quota === true fail-closed, verified empirically); .codex.log persisted; verify/tally returned verbatim; 'mindspec complete' absent (whole-file grep + exact-set test). R9 claim landed bead-1 (68c87106) before the workflow file; attribution test PASSES. All 10 ACs re-run PASS; go build + touched packages green; injection payloads (x$(id), x;id, x|sh, backtick, whitespace, -rf) all rejected at validateArgs or the buildCommand chokepoint in a /tmp node scratch. One non-blocking finding on a mis-specified Validation Proof (below).
findings: NON-BLOCKING oracle mis-specification in the spec, not the implementation: the Validation Proof 'node --check .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js exits 0' cannot pass as written — the workflow necessarily uses top-level await (agent() steps) and a top-level return (R5's result), so node --check fails under both CJS and ESM parsing. The file IS syntactically valid as an async function body (verified: new AsyncFunction('args','agent','pipeline', src) parses clean), which matches the documented workflow runtime shape (args/agent/pipeline globals). This proof was explicitly 'Not a CI gate' and is not an R1-R9 falsification clause, so it does not block; a future doc touch could restate the proof as the async-function-body parse.

validateArgs admits a leading-dash slug/spec ('-rf'), but the buildCommand chokepoint's leading-dash guard rejects it before any command is built (verified empirically) — layered defense holds; noted for completeness since G3's bead-round finding concerned the same guard.
Every shell-metachar/whitespace/control-byte payload on slug/spec/target/bead_id throws; validateMix rejects unknown families at entry; claude_sub_on_quota fail-closes on absent/'true'/1.
Per-bead gate-forward doc-sync verified: 68c87106, 1ad0d796, 84001704/1fd29af9 each append workflow domain docs; .claude/skills mirrors byte-identical to plugin copies.
Branch binary smoke: `mindspec panel create|verify|tally` present in 111's base, `runner: claude-code-skills` remains the default — the dispatch consumes 109/110 contracts unmodified.
